title: Kaworus remains
wikitext:
[]: Kaworu's remains             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Feb 14, 2014 12:57 pm So, after Eva 13 fell, nobody ever bothered to eject Kaworu's plug. There was a theory elsewhere that the crew of the Wunder was going to try to salvage the unit. What would happen with Kaworu's bloody remains? Would they be used as a psychological lever against Shinji? Would there be cloning? Or would they just hose down the entry plug and be done with it?

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Fri Feb 14, 2014 1:04 pm I'm sure we'll know the answer when ever final comes out Hopefully...if Anno doesn't forget that is

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Fri Feb 14, 2014 1:06 pm That's fairly obvious; the point of talking about FINAL at this point is to speculate.

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Fri Feb 14, 2014 1:32 pm I personally would like to see a cloned Kaworu under the control of Nerv. Then Shinji has to fight him; as Kaworu would represents Nerv's last line defense. Thus Shinji's choice: Kill a friend and watch him die (again) or he dies!

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Feb 14, 2014 1:39 pm Well, I don't think that the Wunder managed to salvage EVA-13, first Gendo said that awakening EVA-13 was part of his plan(and since he needed an awakened EVA-01 for his plans 14 years ago that mean that an awakened unit is still vital to his plans), so he probably took measures to make sure that WILLE won't put their hands on it(dispatching NS around EVA-13 for example), also if the Wunder really had managed to get Unit 13, it would be alongside Evas 02 and 08 in the heliport, I don't see anywhere else to drop an Evangelion caught in urgency before hightailing out of the place. But speculating that they did manage to salvage it, I think they would probably take out the remaining entry plug to be sure that it won't reactivate due to some backdoor program, then Ritsuko will examine Kaworu's cadaver to extract as much information as possible, and then they'll either cremate the body or keep it frozen in a morgue for further experimentation. I don't think they'll use the cadaver as a lever against Shinji, since they don't even know what happened to him after Mari ejected him, a cadaver don't seem to be a really useful lever anyway(I mean what will they do : threaten to cremate the body if Shinji don't behave?) and that would be really unnecessarily cruel, they are supposed to be the good guys remember.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Fri Feb 14, 2014 2:10 pm I'vs seen a lot of fanart with Shinji holding Kaworu's severed head and crying. . .

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Fri Feb 14, 2014 2:14 pm Ray wrote: I'vs seen a lot of fanart with Shinji holding Kaworu's severed head and crying. . .

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Feb 14, 2014 2:18 pm Ray wrote: I'vs seen a lot of fanart with Shinji holding Kaworu's severed head and crying. . .

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Fri Feb 14, 2014 6:16 pm Kaworu will be back as some metaphysical being to spell out the final message to Shinji in the end. But being brought back from the dead? Nah. Movie is already going to be stuffed with material.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Feb 14, 2014 6:18 pm Kaworu's bodily remains will not be addressed; they're simply of no interest, so the time would be wasted.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Feb 14, 2014 6:24 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: Kaworu will be back as some metaphysical being to spell out the final message to Shinji in the end. But being brought back from the dead? Nah. Movie is already going to be stuffed with material.

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Fri Feb 14, 2014 7:23 pm Hm. I don't think Kaworu's remains will be important, but I could be wrong. There is that shot where they clearly show you his body is intact, but I think it's just to make the scene worse. If you think that that boy that happily played the piano earlier is now sitting in that entry plug, without a head. Ugh... Shinji was lucky to have the entry plug wall covered in blood...what's behind is worse...

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Fri Feb 21, 2014 4:58 pm Someone on another thread said that what to do with Kaworu's remains could be a source of strain between Misato and Shinji in 4.0. If she showed "callousness or even relief over his death", Shinji would likely lose it and try to throttle Misato. Course he would likely either stop before he did anything permanent or be restrained. Did noone in either the original NGE or The Rebuilds think to examine Kaworu's corpse to find out his origins? I mean you finally have the remains of an Angel to examine without the issue of a giant body with poison blood to deal with. Or did Kaworu's Body dissolve into blood like the other angels in Rebuild did when he was killed?

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Feb 21, 2014 5:43 pm ^ In NGE there wasn't anything remaining of Kaworu's body except his head, the rest was the blood smear in EVA-01's hand you see at the end of the episode. Besides NERV didn't had the time to recuperate the head in the lake of LCL and even less to examine it, since the JSSDF launched their assault the day after. In Rebuild it's possible that WILLE examine Kaworu's body... but they first need to recuperate EVA-13 for that first, and I really doubt that Gendo let them do it, since he implied that an awakened Evangelion was still necessary to his plans. As for Kaworu's death straining Shinji and Misato's relationship if the latter shows relief over his death, that could be an excellent opportunity for him to learn to try to see other people's POV instead of just being pissed off because his "only friend just died" : after all, we don't know what Kaworu did during those past 14 years, maybe he didn't just composed piano pieces and was occasionally sent to fight WILLE and had some of their blood on his hands. That way Shinji could see for himself that everything doesn't revolves around him and what he thinks, that as said in EoE, there's as many truths as people and that he shouldn't dismiss them because they aren't of the same opinion. Also, that could be a way for Shinji to begin to learn a little more about the less savory aspects of Kaworu, I always thought there was something fishy with him...

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Sun Feb 23, 2014 6:32 pm I imagine the discussion goes something like this. Misato: Listen you brat! Shinji: No! You listen! Damn you Misato! Damn you to hell! Damn you and everyone else! You and your stupid collar killed Kaworu! You killed Kaworu! YOU KILLED KAWORU! (Cracks glass) . . It should have been me! It should have been. . . me.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Sun Feb 23, 2014 7:11 pm Nope. It would be more like Misato: Listen you brat! Shinji: ... ANNO: Yes, yes... That's what Shinji should be... As for less savory things about Kaworu... Yeah that would really help Shinji. Even the person who tried to help him and reached to him, decieved him. In the end Shinji will learn how utterly meaningless and unimportant his life is in the grand scheme and becomes even less of a person and more of a useless furniture that stands in others people way. But I digress. I highly doubt Anno will bother with Kaworu's remains so most likely this topic will never be touched in FINAL.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Sun Feb 23, 2014 9:19 pm Just a thought. . . anyway some of us would prefer Shinji get Catharsis of some kind.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sun Feb 23, 2014 9:36 pm Wille is so cruel. I could definitely see them using Kaworu's body to try and get ahead.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Sun Feb 23, 2014 10:18 pm Chuckman wrote: Wille is so cruel. I could definitely see them using Kaworu's body to try and get ahead.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Feb 24, 2014 3:27 am Ray wrote: Just a thought. . . anyway some of us would prefer Shinji get Catharsis of some kind.
